methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $60M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Tenna Systems is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Avner Bendheim and Gabriel Bendheim, currently valued at $60M post-money on $13.5M raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $60M current valuation, implying $150M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector comparables at Series A stage—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yielding a median of $300M and a range of $150M to $500M. The peer-median method reflects more mature funding stages, making it a ceiling-oriented input.

Triangulating these methods produces a low of $128M, median of $225M, and high of $345M. The $97M spread reflects meaningful uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation and the degree to which Tenna's defense-sector positioning justifies peer-level multiples at earlier funding maturity. Key unknowns include revenue trajectory, product-market fit evidence, and competitive positioning within the peer set. The current $60M valuation sits below the triangulated range, suggesting either prior-round discount pricing or that new validation is required to support progression into the $225M–$345M band.
